phases.name: Backend team foundation
phases.description: AgeInStatePercentilesDto + BaseMetricsService.ComputeAgeInStatePercentiles helper (D12 cumulative-total-age-at-exit, visit-level) + TeamMetricsService method + team endpoint mirroring cycleTimePercentiles. Drives the 7 team read-API ATs.

steps.name: AgeInStatePercentilesDto + ComputeAgeInStatePercentiles helper + unit tests
criteria: AgeInStatePercentilesDto is a record (string State, IReadOnlyList<PercentileValue> Percentiles); no SampleSize field per DDD-4
ComputeAgeInStatePercentiles is protected on BaseMetricsService; buckets each completed visit by state using exitTransition.TransitionedAt minus item.StartedDate per DDD-1/D12
Percentiles 50/70/85/95 computed via existing PercentileCalculator; values rise across states in workflow order; band values are visit-level not item-level
A state with zero observations is omitted; a multi-visit (re-work) item contributes one observation per state exit
NUnit unit tests assert exact hand-computed per-state percentiles on a 20-item 3-state fixture plus empty/single/multi-visit boundaries
steps.implementation_notes: TEST PARADIGM (mandatory): Unit tests for this step MUST be written as property-based tests using Hypothesis @given strategies + state-delta matchers (assert_state_delta over a declared universe with strict=True). Single-example unit tests are FALLBACK only - document why in a `# bypass:` comment if used. Acceptance scenarios MUST be framed as Property: blocks (quantified preconditions + invariant outcomes) where the contract supports it; classic Scenario: is fallback. Integration and E2E tests REMAIN single-example - they verify wiring, not invariants. Universe declaration is mandatory even when only one slot is checked: the unchanged() predicate covers the rest. STAGE CONTEXT (cascade): NEW code = Stage 0 (paradigm-from-day-zero, no debt accumulates). Bug-prone legacy code = Stage 1 first (state-delta migration captures hidden mutations, 33-75% yield), THEN Stage 2 PBT amplification (low yield by design - surface is debt-paid). Don't expect high hit rate on Stage-1-completed surfaces; that's healthy. See nw-tdd-methodology::Paradigm Mandate for the full debt-payoff framing. EXEMPT FROM PARADIGM: this is a C#/.NET + NUnit project (the C#-row of the polyglot matrix governs, not the Python/Hypothesis pilot per the DISTILL Infrastructure Policy note) - Hypothesis/assert_state_delta do NOT apply. Compensate with exact-value example-based NUnit unit tests on a hand-computed fixture (the strongest available oracle for the percentile math) and the visit-level/rise-left-to-right invariant assertions. NEW AgeInStatePercentilesDto (WHY-NEW-FILE: zero existing overlap per the Reuse-Analysis grep for AgeInState|PerStatePercentile; CLOSEST-EXISTING the existing metrics DTOs reuse PercentileValue but none carry a per-state shape). EXTEND BaseMetricsService with the protected ComputeAgeInStatePercentiles helper per DDD-9 (intra-inheritance, NOT an interface - ADR-021 forbids IPerStateAggregationService). Algorithm per ADR-019 + D12: for the supplied completed-items-in-window, walk transitions via IWorkItemStateTransitionRepository.GetAllByPredicate(t => completedItemIds.Contains(t.WorkItemId)) (or an EF-friendly composition; crafter chooses at GREEN), record cumulative total age at exit = exitTransition.TransitionedAt - item.StartedDate for each Doing-category state the item left, bucket by state in workflow order, compute via PercentileCalculator.CalculatePercentile per 50/70/85/95. REUSE-AS-IS PercentileCalculator, PercentileValue, IWorkItemStateTransitionRepository (no change). Mind the slice-01 mapped-state lesson: compare/bucket on the mapped ToState. NEW BaseMetricsServiceTests methods (or alongside TeamMetricsServiceTests): exact 50/70/85/95 per state on a 20-completed-item 3-state fixture (In Progress/Review/Test) with known StartedDate + transition timestamps; assert monotonic rise left-to-right; boundary: empty state omitted, single-observation state, multi-visit re-work yields one observation per exit; visit-level (not item-level) sampling assertion per the ADR-019 enforcement table. Immutability (ImmutableList/record, no mutable List<T> state), <=2 nesting, no else chains, switch expressions, no comments. Nullable enabled, TreatWarningsAsErrors - zero-warning build.

steps.name: TeamMetricsService method + cache wrap + team endpoint
criteria: ITeamMetricsService declares GetAgeInStatePercentilesForTeam(Team, DateTime startDate, DateTime endDate); TeamMetricsService implements it

Implementation uses existing GetWorkItemsClosedInDateRange (DDD-2 membership) then ComputeAgeInStatePercentiles, wrapped in GetFromCacheIfExists with key AgeInStatePercentiles_{start:yyyy-MM-dd}_{end:yyyy-MM-dd}
TeamMetricsController exposes GET ageInStatePercentiles; startDate.Date > endDate.Date returns 400 with StartDateMustBeBeforeEndDateErrorMessage
Workflow state order comes from the team's existing doingStates; default percentiles 50/70/85/95
All 7 team ATs in AgeInStatePercentilesReadApiIntegrationTest are un-ignored and go green
steps.implementation_notes: TEST PARADIGM (mandatory): Unit tests for this step MUST be written as property-based tests using Hypothesis @given strategies + state-delta matchers (assert_state_delta over a declared universe with strict=True). Integration and E2E tests REMAIN single-example - they verify wiring, not invariants. Universe declaration is mandatory even when only one slot is checked: the unchanged() predicate covers the rest. STAGE CONTEXT (cascade): NEW code = Stage 0 (paradigm-from-day-zero, no debt accumulates). Bug-prone legacy code = Stage 1 first (state-delta migration captures hidden mutations, 33-75% yield), THEN Stage 2 PBT amplification (low yield by design - surface is debt-paid). Don't expect high hit rate on Stage-1-completed surfaces; that's healthy. See nw-tdd-methodology::Paradigm Mandate for the full debt-payoff framing. EXEMPT FROM PARADIGM: the un-ignored ATs are layer-4 read-API integration tests (WebApplicationFactory + real EF) that verify wiring, not invariants - they REMAIN single-example per the mandate. The invariant coverage (exact math, rise, visit-level) lives in the 01-01 unit tests on the adjacent ComputeAgeInStatePercentiles helper. EXTEND ITeamMetricsService + TeamMetricsService mirroring GetCycleTimePercentilesForTeam (TeamMetricsService.cs:303-320): (1) GetWorkItemsClosedInDateRange(team, startDate, endDate) - same predicate the existing CT path uses per DDD-2; (2) call the 01-01 ComputeAgeInStatePercentiles helper passing the team's doingStates as the workflow order and [50,70,85,95]; (3) wrap in GetFromCacheIfExists(team, $"AgeInStatePercentiles_{startDate:yyyy-MM-dd}_{endDate:yyyy-MM-dd}", () => ..., logger) per DDD-5 (REUSE-AS-IS cache, new key namespace alongside CycleTimePercentiles_). EXTEND TeamMetricsController mirroring GetCycleTimePercentilesForTeam (line 119-129): [HttpGet("ageInStatePercentiles")] returning ActionResult<IEnumerable<AgeInStatePercentilesDto>>, validation startDate.Date > endDate.Date => BadRequest(StartDateMustBeBeforeEndDateErrorMessage) reusing the existing in-class const (verified at TeamMetricsController.cs:19), dispatch via GetEntityByIdAnExecuteAction(teamRepository, teamId, team => teamMetricsService.GetAgeInStatePercentilesForTeam(team, startDate, endDate)). Auth via the existing class-level [RbacGuard(TeamRead)] - inherited, do NOT add a new guard; verify presence at GREEN. RED handoff from DISTILL (ADR-025: RED only unskips DISTILL scaffolds): un-ignore the 7 ATs in AgeInStatePercentilesReadApiIntegrationTest ONE at a time and confirm RED-for-right-reason per the DISTILL Pre-DELIVER gate table - #3/#4/#5/#8 fail because the route is absent (404) or the body is empty/wrong; #6 (empty team) and #7 (inverted-range 400) and #9 (RBAC) may PASS the moment the route+validation+inherited guard exist - that is EXPECTED, keep them as regression guards. WRONG RED = JsonDocument.Parse throws on an error page / 500 / seed not saved -> BLOCK and fix the setup, not the production gap. Post-GREEN Fixture-Theater check: git diff --stat MUST show AgeInStatePercentilesDto.cs + BaseMetricsService.cs + TeamMetricsService.cs + TeamMetricsController.cs changed - if only the test file changed the percentiles are echoed by the seed not computed; BLOCK. Immutability, <=2 nesting, no else, switch expressions, no comments. Async all the way (CancellationToken through). Zero-warning build.

phases.name: Portfolio parity
phases.description: PortfolioMetricsService method + PortfolioMetricsController endpoint mirroring the team path, scoped to the portfolio's completed-features-in-window (D8). Drives the 2 portfolio read-API ATs.

steps.name: PortfolioMetricsService method + portfolio endpoint
criteria: IPortfolioMetricsService declares GetAgeInStatePercentilesForPortfolio(Portfolio, DateTime startDate, DateTime endDate); PortfolioMetricsService implements it via the same pattern as Team
Completed items in window come from the portfolio's existing closed-in-range query; delegates to the shared ComputeAgeInStatePercentiles helper with the same cache-key shape
PortfolioMetricsController exposes GET ageInStatePercentiles mirroring the team endpoint validation and dispatch; auth via inherited RbacGuard(PortfolioRead)
Band values rise across the portfolio workflow states (Analyzing/Building/Validating) per D12
Both portfolio ATs in AgeInStatePercentilesPortfolioReadApiIntegrationTest are un-ignored and go green
steps.implementation_notes: TEST PARADIGM (mandatory): Unit tests for this step MUST be written as property-based tests using Hypothesis @given strategies + state-delta matchers (assert_state_delta over a declared universe with strict=True). EXTEND PortfolioMetricsController mirroring its existing cycleTimePercentiles endpoint (PortfolioMetricsController.cs:100-114): [HttpGet("ageInStatePercentiles")], same startDate.Date > endDate.Date => BadRequest validation (PortfolioMetricsController has its own equivalent error const for its existing endpoints - reuse it, no new const), GetEntityByIdAnExecuteAction(portfolioRepository, portfolioId, ...). WRONG RED = Parse throws / 500 -> BLOCK. Immutability, <=2 nesting, no else, switch expressions, async with CancellationToken, no comments. Zero-warning build.

phases.name: Architecture enforcement rules
phases.description: ArchUnitNET rules per ADR-021: no *PerStateAggregation* type exists; metrics services read transitions only via IWorkItemStateTransitionRepository never DbSet directly; ComputeAgeInStatePercentiles is protected (intra-inheritance only).

steps.name: ArchUnitNET rules for the per-state aggregation seam
criteria: An ArchUnitNET rule asserts no class or interface named *PerStateAggregation* exists in the assembly (ADR-021 / DDD-10 upheld)
A rule asserts the metrics-service classes read WorkItemStateTransition only via IWorkItemStateTransitionRepository, never via DbSet<WorkItemStateTransition> (extends the ADR-015 rule)
A rule asserts BaseMetricsService.ComputeAgeInStatePercentiles is protected (not public, not an interface member), preventing accidental promotion to a shared service
The new rules are added to the existing architecture test suite and pass
dotnet build stays zero-warning and the full backend suite stays green
steps.implementation_notes: TEST PARADIGM (mandatory): Unit tests for this step MUST be written as property-based tests using Hypothesis @given strategies + state-delta matchers (assert_state_delta over a declared universe with strict=True). Single-example unit tests are FALLBACK only - document why in a `# bypass:` comment if used. Acceptance scenarios MUST be framed as Property: blocks (quantified preconditions + invariant outcomes) where the contract supports it; classic Scenario: is fallback. Integration and E2E tests REMAIN single-example - they verify wiring, not invariants. Universe declaration is mandatory even when only one slot is checked: the unchanged() predicate covers the rest. STAGE CONTEXT (cascade): NEW code = Stage 0 (paradigm-from-day-zero, no debt accumulates). See nw-tdd-methodology::Paradigm Mandate for the full debt-payoff framing. EXEMPT FROM PARADIGM: ArchUnitNET rules are structural assertions over the type graph (the C#-row equivalent of import-linter contracts); they are inherently invariant-style (the rule IS the universal-quantifier over all types) but expressed in the ArchUnitNET fluent API, not Hypothesis. Compensate: each rule is itself the property ("for all types T, NOT named *PerStateAggregation*"; "for all metrics services, transitions accessed only via the repository port"; "ComputeAgeInStatePercentiles has protected visibility"). EXTEND the existing architecture suite (find the existing ArchUnitNET test class - the slice-01 TimeInStateSeamArchUnitTest established the pattern; if a metrics-specific class is the better home create MetricsArchitectureTests, else add methods to the existing suite - WHY-NEW-FILE only if no metrics architecture class exists, CLOSEST-EXISTING the slice-01 seam ArchUnit class). Three rules per ADR-021: (1) Types().That().HaveNameContaining("PerStateAggregation") => Should().NotExist() (upholds ADR-018/DDD-10 - the repository IS the shared seam, no aggregation service); (2) the metrics-service classes (BaseMetricsService/TeamMetricsService/PortfolioMetricsService) depend on IWorkItemStateTransitionRepository and do NOT depend on DbSet<WorkItemStateTransition> directly (extends the ADR-015 transitions-via-repository-only rule to the new readers); (3) ComputeAgeInStatePercentiles is protected - assert via reflection/ArchUnitNET member visibility that the method is not public and BaseMetricsService exposes no public per-state aggregation member (intra-inheritance only per DDD-9). This is a pure architecture-rule step - no production code changes; if any rule fails at first run, the FIX is in the production code from phases 01/02 (e.g. accidental public helper), not a rule relaxation. Sonar: keep cognitive complexity low (one assert per rule, EnterMultipleScope if grouped per NUnit2045). No comments. Zero-warning build.

phases.name: Frontend data plumbing + chart overlay + toggle chip
phases.description: IPerStatePercentileValues model, MetricsService.getAgeInStatePercentiles, useMetricsData parallel fetch + new ctx field, BaseMetricsView prop, WorkItemAgingChart <rect> band overlay (off by default, behind dots), PercentileLegend single Pace percentiles chip. Vitest authored here. PLUS 04-04: extend CsvWorkTrackingConnector to synthesize a multi-state per-state journey + enrich scenario-0 demo CSVs so the live walking skeleton renders bands.

steps.name: TS model + MetricsService.getAgeInStatePercentiles + useMetricsData ctx
criteria: IPerStatePercentileValues { state: string; percentiles: IPercentileValue[] } mirrors the backend DTO; no sampleSize field per DDD-4
IMetricsService and MetricsService gain getAgeInStatePercentiles(id, startDate, endDate) mirroring getCycleTimePercentiles GET shape
useMetricsData fetches it in parallel in the same effect as getCycleTimePercentiles and returns perStatePercentileValues (default [])
Vitest covers MetricsService resolving the new endpoint and useMetricsData populating ctx.perStatePercentileValues alongside the existing fetch
pnpm build is clean and Biome reports zero warnings on ./src
steps.scenario_name: getAgeInStatePercentiles fetches per-state percentile values for an entity and window
steps.implementation_notes: TEST PARADIGM (mandatory): Unit tests for this step MUST be written as property-based tests using Hypothesis @given strategies + state-delta matchers (assert_state_delta over a declared universe with strict=True). Single-example unit tests are FALLBACK only - document why in a `# bypass:` comment if used. Acceptance scenarios MUST be framed as Property: blocks (quantified preconditions + invariant outcomes) where the contract supports it; classic Scenario: is fallback. Integration and E2E tests REMAIN single-example - they verify wiring, not invariants. Universe declaration is mandatory even when only one slot is checked: the unchanged() predicate covers the rest. STAGE CONTEXT (cascade): NEW code = Stage 0 (paradigm-from-day-zero, no debt accumulates). See nw-tdd-methodology::Paradigm Mandate for the full debt-payoff framing. EXEMPT FROM PARADIGM: React/TypeScript + Vitest project (the TS-row of the polyglot matrix governs, not the Python/Hypothesis pilot); Hypothesis/assert_state_delta do NOT apply. These are service-fetch + hook-wiring unit tests that verify wiring, not invariants - single-example per the mandate. NEW IPerStatePercentileValues (PerStatePercentileValues.ts) composing the existing IPercentileValue (REUSE-AS-IS, no change to PercentileValue.ts); WHY-NEW-FILE: zero existing overlap per the Reuse-Analysis grep; no sampleSize (DDD-4). EXTEND IMetricsService + MetricsService.getAgeInStatePercentiles mirroring getCycleTimePercentiles (MetricsService.ts:247-260): GET `/${this.api}/${id}/metrics/ageInStatePercentiles?${this.getDateFormatString(startDate, endDate)}` returning Promise<IPerStatePercentileValues[]>. Mock only abstractions you own - do NOT mock fetch/axios directly (CLAUDE.md); test through the service's own seam as the existing MetricsService tests do. EXTEND useMetricsData (useMetricsData.ts ~line 211): add the parallel metricsService.getAgeInStatePercentiles(entity.id, startDate, endDate) call in the SAME effect that calls getCycleTimePercentiles (Promise.all shape - crafter chooses), add perStatePercentileValues: IPerStatePercentileValues[] to the returned ctx defaulting to []. CRITICAL (project memory project_react185_loop_unstable_useeffect_dep): do NOT introduce a new inline new Date() / unstable object into a useEffect dependency array - reuse the existing window state deps; if a derived value feeds a dep, useMemo-stabilise it. Schema-first at the trust boundary: if MetricsService deserialises responses via a Zod schema, extend that schema and derive the type via z.infer (do NOT as-cast). Vitest: factory getMockX(overrides?) for the per-state payload validated against IPerStatePercentileValues; assert the service resolves the parsed shape and the hook surfaces ctx.perStatePercentileValues; assert the existing percentileValues fetch is unaffected (no regression). Strict TS, no any (narrow unknown with a guard). Biome clean. pnpm build zero warnings.

steps.name: WorkItemAgingChart rect band overlay + BaseMetricsView wiring
criteria: WorkItemAgingChart accepts optional perStatePercentileValues prop; absent/empty renders today-identical, guarded by the no-regression default-off snapshot
When showPaceBands is on, each state with data renders stacked SVG <rect> zones spanning x in [stateIndex-0.4, stateIndex+0.4] across consecutive percentile boundaries, green to red, behind the dots
showPaceBands is a single local boolean state off by default; useChartVisibility is unchanged; the existing CT lines and SLE line are unaffected
BaseMetricsView passes perStatePercentileValues={ctx.perStatePercentileValues} to the chart in both team and portfolio scope
Vitest covers default-off no-rect snapshot, overlay-on rect x-span/y-boundary/green-to-red fill, and z-order behind the dots
steps.scenario_name: renders per-state pace band rects behind the dots only when the overlay is toggled on
steps.implementation_notes: TEST PARADIGM (mandatory): Unit tests for this step MUST be written as property-based tests using Hypothesis @given strategies + state-delta matchers (assert_state_delta over a declared universe with strict=True). Single-example unit tests are FALLBACK only - document why in a `# bypass:` comment if used. Acceptance scenarios MUST be framed as Property: blocks where the contract supports it; classic Scenario: is fallback. Integration and E2E tests REMAIN single-example. Universe declaration is mandatory even when only one slot is checked: the unchanged() predicate covers the rest. STAGE CONTEXT (cascade): NEW code = Stage 0 (paradigm-from-day-zero, no debt accumulates). See nw-tdd-methodology::Paradigm Mandate for the full debt-payoff framing. EXEMPT FROM PARADIGM: React/TypeScript + Vitest/RTL project (TS-row of the polyglot matrix); Hypothesis does NOT apply. SVG-rendering + snapshot + toggle behaviours are example-based RTL assertions (golden-snapshot diff + DOM presence), not quantified invariants. Compensate: the no-regression default-off SNAPSHOT is the golden-file oracle; the band-rendering test asserts exact x-span/y-boundary geometry. DELIVER SPIKE FIRST (~30 min, DESIGN open question): confirm an SVG <rect> child of <ChartsContainer> receives data-space coordinates (like <ChartsReferenceLine>) so x in [stateIndex-0.4, stateIndex+0.4] and percentile-value Y boundaries place correctly; if false, fall back to a getBoundingClientRect()-positioned <svg> overlay (heavier, still feasible) - no DESIGN blocker. EXTEND WorkItemAgingChart per ADR-020 + DDD-6: add optional perStatePercentileValues?: IPerStatePercentileValues[] prop; render the <rect> filled-zone overlay as a child of the existing <ChartsContainer> BEFORE the dots (z-order = drawn first = behind); for each state with data, one stacked set of rects at the consecutive percentile boundaries (0->p50->p70->p85->p95->top) filled from the green->red ForecastLevel(percentile).color palette (REUSE-AS-IS ForecastLevel.ts, already imported). Extract the half-width as `const STATE_BAND_HALF_WIDTH = 0.4` (Review-gate carry-forward L: magic-number extraction). Gate the whole overlay on a single showPaceBands boolean - a trivial local useState in WorkItemAgingChart, off by default (DDD-8); do NOT touch useChartVisibility (DDD-8/Reuse-Analysis NO CHANGE) and do NOT add a per-percentile visibility map, tooltip annotation, or per-band hover (US-03 + per-band hover CUT). EXTEND BaseMetricsView (line 788-794) to pass perStatePercentileValues={ctx.perStatePercentileValues} to the <WorkItemAgingChart> - this single shared edit covers BOTH team and portfolio routes (do NOT special-case scope). Vitest + RTL: (a) no-regression: render with perStatePercentileValues present but overlay off -> assert NO band rects, DOM matches the existing snapshot (DoD #3 no-regression); (b) band rendering: 2-state fixture + overlay on -> assert the expected <rect>s at expected x-span/y-boundaries with green->red fills; (c) z-order: assert the rect group precedes the dot series in DOM order. Skip framework renders-without-crashing tests (CLAUDE.md). Factory getMockX(overrides?) for the per-state fixture. Strict TS, no any. Biome clean. pnpm build zero warnings.

steps.name: PercentileLegend single Pace percentiles toggle chip
criteria: PercentileLegend gains optional showPaceBands, onTogglePaceBands, and paceBandsAvailable props and renders one chip labelled Pace percentiles when band data exists
The chip is off by default; clicking it toggles the overlay; clicking again hides it; the existing CT percentile chips and SLE chip are unaffected
No second chip group, no sub-headers, no low-sample notice; the chip is hidden when no state has band data (paceBandsAvailable false)
Vitest covers chip-off-by-default, click-on then click-off, the existing chips unaffected, and chip absent when no band data
pnpm build is clean and Biome reports zero warnings on ./src
steps.scenario_name: renders one off-by-default Pace percentiles chip that toggles the overlay without affecting existing chips
steps.implementation_notes: TEST PARADIGM (mandatory): Unit tests for this step MUST be written as property-based tests using Hypothesis @given strategies + state-delta matchers (assert_state_delta over a declared universe with strict=True). Single-example unit tests are FALLBACK only - document why in a `# bypass:` comment if used. Acceptance scenarios MUST be framed as Property: blocks where the contract supports it; classic Scenario: is fallback. Integration and E2E tests REMAIN single-example. Universe declaration is mandatory even when only one slot is checked: the unchanged() predicate covers the rest. STAGE CONTEXT (cascade): NEW code = Stage 0 (paradigm-from-day-zero, no debt accumulates). See nw-tdd-methodology::Paradigm Mandate for the full debt-payoff framing. EXEMPT FROM PARADIGM: React/TypeScript + Vitest/RTL project (TS-row of the polyglot matrix); Hypothesis does NOT apply. Chip presence/toggle/independence are example-based RTL interaction assertions (click -> DOM delta), not quantified invariants. Compensate: parametrise the (initial-state, click-count) -> expected-overlay-state cases. EXTEND PercentileLegend per DDD-8 + Reuse-Analysis (net ~8 LOC): add optional showPaceBands?: boolean, onTogglePaceBands?: () => void, paceBandsAvailable?: boolean; render ONE chip labelled exactly 'Pace percentiles' (the E2E POM pins this label) styled consistently with the existing chips, rendered only when paceBandsAvailable is true (>=1 state has band data per DDD-4/the omitted-state rule). No second chip group, no sub-headers, no low-sample notice (all CUT 2026-05-25). EXTEND WorkItemAgingChart to wire the legend chip to the 04-02 showPaceBands local state (pass showPaceBands + onTogglePaceBands={() => setShowPaceBands(v => !v)} + paceBandsAvailable={perStatePercentileValues.length > 0} into <PercentileLegend>) - this is the small follow-on edit to the same chart file from 04-02 (kept in a separate step so the legend chip is its own RED->GREEN cycle; the chart file appears in both 04-02 and 04-03 files_to_modify by design). Do NOT touch useChartVisibility (DDD-8 NO CHANGE) - the existing percentile/SLE wiring stays untouched and the new chip is purely additive/independent (US-02 AC). Vitest + RTL: chip off by default (no overlay-on signal); click -> onTogglePaceBands fires / overlay-on; click again -> off; assert the existing CT percentile chips + SLE chip count and behaviour are unchanged throughout (US-02 AC); chip ABSENT when paceBandsAvailable is false. Skip renders-without-crashing framework tests. Strict TS, no any. Biome clean. pnpm build zero warnings.

steps.name: Demo data per-state journey (CSV multi-state transitions)
criteria: CsvWorkTrackingConnector synthesizes an ordered From->To transition chain from per-state entered-date columns (workflow order), not just a single FromState-empty entered transition
The existing single state-entered-date behaviour (time-in-state CurrentStateEnteredAt) is preserved with zero regression when only one column is present
Demo CSVs for scenario-0's team and portfolio carry per-state entered dates so completed items have a multi-state journey
An integration test seeded from the enriched demo data asserts GET ageInStatePercentiles returns >=1 state with a non-empty rising band for the demo team and portfolio
dotnet build zero-warning; full backend suite green
steps.implementation_notes: BLOCKER RESOLUTION for 05-01 (live walking skeleton returned empty bands on demo data). Root cause: CsvWorkTrackingConnector.BuildStateEnteredTransitions (CsvWorkTrackingConnector.cs:207-230) emits at most ONE synthetic transition per item with FromState=string.Empty (only for Doing-category items with a state-entered-date column) - sufficient for time-in-state's CurrentStateEnteredAt but NOT for the pace-band metric which buckets age-at-exit by the transition FromState across a multi-state journey. EXTEND the connector to read per-state entered-date columns and synthesize the ordered From->To chain (e.g. entered IP at T0, Review at T1, Test at T2 -> transitions (empty->IP,T0),(IP->Review,T1),(Review->Test,T2); the exit of the last doing state is the Done/Closed date). Choose a clean column-naming convention (mirror/extend the existing single StateEnteredDateHeader option via CsvWorkTrackingOptionNames; the sibling slice-03 added the single state-since column - this generalises it to per-state). PRESERVE the single-column path (regression: time-in-state demo still derives CurrentStateEnteredAt). ENRICH the demo CSVs that scenario 0 uses (the team + the portfolio the E2E navigates to) with per-state entered dates for completed items so bands render; other demo files MAY follow but scenario-0 coverage is the bar. Verify via an integration test in the DemoDataStateEnteredSeamIntegrationTest style (real DemoDataFactory/DemoDataService + the ageInStatePercentiles read path) asserting non-empty rising bands. Immutability, <=2 nesting, switch expressions, no comments, zero-warning build. This is a NEW TDD cycle (RED a failing demo-data integration test that wants non-empty bands -> GREEN the connector + CSV enrichment). After this step, re-dispatch 05-01 (the live E2E) - it should then render the chip + bands and pass.

phases.name: Walking skeletons (live Playwright)
phases.description: Un-fixme the 2 held E2E walking-skeleton scenarios (team + portfolio toggle), run each live against a locally-started app per the project rule using the existing POM, and drive them green. Confirm the pace-band data-testid and Pace percentiles chip label match what was shipped.

steps.name: Un-fixme and run the 2 walking-skeleton scenarios live
criteria: Both test.fixme markers in AgingPacePercentiles.spec.ts (team + portfolio) are removed and the scenarios run in the E2E suite
On the team aging chart: no bands by default, toggle the Pace percentiles chip on, pace-band rects appear behind the dots, toggle off, bands gone; cycle-time chips unchanged
The same toggle and band behaviour holds on the portfolio aging chart (D8 parity) via the shared chart
The widget id aging, chip label Pace percentiles, and data-testid pace-band match what DELIVER shipped; all element access stays in the POM
Each scenario was run against a locally-started app loaded with demo scenario 0 before commit and passes
steps.scenario_name: flow coach toggles per-state pace bands on and off on the team aging chart
steps.implementation_notes: EXEMPT FROM PARADIGM: E2E walking-skeleton (single-shot UI flow over the production app); the mandate keeps E2E single-example - they verify wiring, not invariants. The per-state percentile-math invariants live in the 01-01 backend unit tests and the 01-02/02-01 read-API ATs (faster, deterministic) per the established sibling pattern. WS strategy Type A (additive). The 2 scenarios + the POM are already committed (test.fixme): the spec drives demo data via loadDemoScenario(0) + waitForBackgroundUpdates -> goToTeam/goToPortfolio -> goToMetrics -> switchCategory(FlowMetrics) -> getWidgetByName(aging) and uses the POM WorkItemAgingChart.ts which pins data-testid="pace-band", the 'Pace percentiles' chip label, and the cycle-time chip count reader (all present). USER/AGENT RUNS LIVE per project memory (feedback_e2e_run_locally_by_user: Claude runs the live Playwright itself - creds in env, do NOT punt to the user; feedback_run_playwright_before_commit: never commit a spec/POM not RUN against a locally-started app, pnpm build only type-checks; feedback_e2e_page_object_model: POM-only, no inline page.locator; feedback_e2e_use_demo_data: drive from seeded demo data, never live connector syncs). Procedure: un-fixme ONE scenario at a time, start the app locally (dotnet run backend + frontend), load demo scenario 0, run that scenario, confirm it FIRST fails for the right reason (no 'Pace percentiles' chip / no pace-band rects render before the 01-04 production code is in place - though by this step phases 01-04 are done, so confirm green) then green; only adjust a POM if a real accessible name / testid differs from what DELIVER shipped (POM-only - never inline page.locator). The portfolio scenario hits the portfolio metrics read path (no LicenseGuard on a GET metrics endpoint, but if the running app gates any portfolio premium surface apply the premium-license dev seed per memory reference_premium_license_dev_seed: dotnet run + POST /api/v1/license/import with valid_not_expired_license.json + verify canUsePremiumFeatures). ORCHESTRATOR NOTE: this step REQUIRES A RUNNING APP - it cannot be self-certified from a type-check; the agent runs the live Playwright and reports the live-run result. Biome/lint clean on the E2E project. Confirm at GREEN the widget id 'aging', chip label 'Pace percentiles', and data-testid 'pace-band' match exactly what 04-02/04-03 shipped (the POM pins all three).

validation.note: 1 low: scenario_name is singular while 01-02 (7 team ATs), 02-01 (2 portfolio ATs), 05-01 (2 E2E) each drive multiple scenarios. Auditability nicety, not a blocker. All 11 DISTILL scenarios covered.
